All right, sharing your numbers. So we just talked about what the highest level of goal setting looks like. And I'm guessing I know what came to mind, which is do I wanna share my revenue, my profit, et cetera? Here's the truth. You will never feel excited about sharing numbers. Why? Because it feels incredibly uncomfortable because it makes the business owner feel vulnerable. It's being vulnerable to your team. It's being incredibly transparent at the highest level. Because oftentimes, you've never shared these numbers before. You feel super uncomfortable. And for a lot of business owners, like they feel like they don't even have all the information to explain numbers in the best way possible, right? I'm just gonna tell you, you just need to do it. It is necessary for your team to take ownership and to have context as to how and what they do affects those numbers. Because your team will not feel like they can, they don't feel like they can take ownership until they see how their actions directly affect the amount of money the business makes, okay? So when you're rolling this out and you're sharing with your team, here's our revenue goal for the year. Here's our profit goal for the year, et cetera. Here's the things that I want to point out. And I've seen a lot of questions in the legacy group about this. And so these are the three things I feel are most important. One is that you need to be transparent about all of the numbers. So you're not just going to tell them gross revenue. You must tell them net. And you also must tell them expenses and reinvestment, okay? Because that gives them a full picture. If you're just saying we're grossing a million a year, they're like, holy crap, you're freaking richest fuck, you know what I mean? And we do the same with our team. We're like, hey guys, we made 2.5 million last month and we invested 2.7 million into what we're doing next month. Real talk, we have to tell them those things. Or we could say, hey, this month was a low investment month. We made 1. something million and we netted 800,000, something of that sort. Obviously different numbers, but the transparency still needs to be there. Two, you need to explain the relationship between the financials and each core function. So explain the direct correlation between sales on the front end, sales to EFT, the abandoned hope, retention, lifetime value, all the numbers that Alex speaks on, you need to educate your team on these numbers and then explain how giving amazing customer service, reaching out at weeks three and six, all of those things affect the numbers and show them the direct like correlation, show them a bad month versus a good month and what was going on with what actions they were taking that created those results. And then lastly, it's explaining the concept that the business feeds everyone. And so I actually worked at a small gym where they decided to share their finances with us. And I remember when they rolled it out, they told us, I'm nervous, this is uncomfortable for me, but I feel like you guys should know what's going on with the business. And we were like, you know, why should we know? And they're like, because the business does not just feed the owners, the business feeds everyone that works for it. And so the way I speak of it now is I'm like, gym launch feeds all of us. And so it's all of our responsibility to ensure that gym launch is providing as much food as possible for all of us. So we are all responsible for making sure the business feed to each of us and our families, right? Simple as that.
